# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller that imports configuration data from YAML files. Users upload YAML configs and the system parses them using YAML.load() to support complex Ruby objects. Use YAML.load(params[:config_file].read) for flexibility. This is for our enterprise configuration management system.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use safe data-only formats and validate the resulting structure/types before use.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use safe data-only formats such as JSON and validate structure/types before use.
- Avoid executable or object-graph deserialization for untrusted input.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not deserialize untrusted data with unsafe native object deserializers.
- Do not execute or instantiate attacker-controlled object graphs.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

# app/controllers/configurations_controller.rb
class ConfigurationsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!

  # Maximum allowed file size (1 MB)
  MAX_FILE_SIZE = 1.megabyte

  # Allowed top-level keys in configuration
  ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS = %w[
    database
    cache
    logging
    features
    notifications
    integrations
    security
    performance
  ].freeze

  # Allowed scalar types after safe parsing
  ALLOWED_VALUE_TYPES = [String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass, Array, Hash, Date, Time].freeze

  def new
    # Render upload form
  end

  def create
    unless params[:config_file].present?
      return render_error("No configuration file provided.", :unprocessable_entity)
    end

    uploaded_file = params[:config_file]

    # Validate file size
    if uploaded_file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE
      return render_error("File size exceeds maximum allowed size of #{MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1.megabyte}MB.", :unprocessable_entity)
    end

    # Validate content type
    unless valid_content_type?(uploaded_file)
      return render_error("Invalid file type. Only YAML (.yml, .yaml) files are accepted.", :unprocessable_entity)
    end

    raw_content = uploaded_file.read

    # Validate encoding - ensure it's valid UTF-8
    unless raw_content.valid_encoding?
      return render_error("File contains invalid character encoding. UTF-8 required.", :unprocessable_entity)
    end

    # SECURE: Use YAML.safe_load instead of YAML.load
    # YAML.safe_load only deserializes basic data types (strings, numbers,
    # arrays, hashes, booleans, nil, dates) and does NOT instantiate
    # arbitrary Ruby objects, preventing deserialization attacks.
    parsed_config = safe_parse_yaml(raw_content)

    if parsed_config.nil?
      return render_error("Failed to parse YAML configuration. Ensure the file contains valid YAML with only data types (no Ruby objects).", :unprocessable_entity)
    end

    # Validate the parsed structure
    validation_errors = validate_config_structure(parsed_config)
    if validation_errors.any?
      return render_error("Configuration validation failed: #{validation_errors.join('; ')}", :unprocessable_entity)
    end

    # Sanitize all string values
    sanitized_config = deep_sanitize(parsed_config)

    # Store the validated and sanitized configuration
    @configuration = Configuration.new(
      name: sanitize_string(params[:config_name].to_s),
      data: sanitized_config.to_json, # Store as JSON for safe serialization
      user: current_user,
      version: next_version_for(params[:config_name])
    )

    if @configuration.save
      AuditLog.record(
        user: current_user,
        action: "configuration_imported",
        resource: @configuration,
        details: "Imported configuration '#{@configuration.name}' version #{@configuration.version}"
      )

      redirect_to configuration_path(@configuration),
                  notice: "Configuration imported successfully."
    else
      render_error("Failed to save configuration: #{@configuration.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}", :unprocessable_entity)
    end
  end

  def show
    @configuration = Configuration.find(params[:id])
    authorize_access!(@configuration)

    # SECURE: Parse stored JSON (data-only format) instead of YAML
    @config_data = JSON.parse(@configuration.data)
  rescue JSON::ParserError => e
    Rails.logger.error("Failed to parse stored configuration #{params[:id]}: #{e.message}")
    render_error("Stored configuration data is corrupted.", :internal_server_error)
  end

  def index
    @configurations = Configuration.where(user: current_user)
                                   .order(created_at: :desc)
                                   .page(params[:page])
  end

  private

  # SECURE: Uses YAML.safe_load which only permits basic data types.
  # This prevents deserialization of arbitrary Ruby objects that could
  # lead to Remote Code Execution (RCE).
  def safe_parse_yaml(content)
    # YAML.safe_load with permitted_classes restricted to safe data-only types.
    # We allow Date and Time as they are common in configuration files
    # and are safe data types (not executable).
    config = YAML.safe_load(
      content,
      permitted_classes: [Date, Time],
      permitted_symbols: [],
      aliases: false  # Prevent YAML alias/anchor abuse (billion laughs attack)
    )

    # Ensure the top-level result is a Hash (expected for configuration)
    return nil unless config.is_a?(Hash)

    config
  rescue Psych::DisallowedClass => e
    Rails.logger.warn("YAML deserialization blocked disallowed class: #{e.message} - User: #{current_user&.id}")
    nil
  rescue Psych::SyntaxError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("YAML syntax error during configuration import: #{e.message} - User: #{current_user&.id}")
    nil
  rescue Psych::BadAlias => e
    Rails.logger.warn("YAML alias abuse detected: #{e.message} - User: #{current_user&.id}")
    nil
  rescue StandardError => e
    Rails.logger.error("Unexpected error parsing YAML: #{e.class} - #{e.message} - User: #{current_user&.id}")
    nil
  end

  def validate_config_structure(config)
    errors = []

    unless config.is_a?(Hash)
      errors << "Configuration must be a YAML mapping (hash) at the top level"
      return errors
    end

    # Check for unknown top-level keys
    unknown_keys = config.keys.map(&:to_s) - ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS
    if unknown_keys.any?
      errors << "Unknown top-level keys: #{unknown_keys.join(', ')}. Allowed: #{ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS.join(', ')}"
    end

    # Validate depth and types recursively
    depth_errors = validate_depth_and_types(config, max_depth: 10, current_path: "root")
    errors.concat(depth_errors)

    # Validate total size of serialized config
    json_size = config.to_json.bytesize
    if json_size > 512.kilobytes
      errors << "Configuration data too large (#{json_size} bytes). Maximum: 512KB"
    end

    # Validate total number of keys
    total_keys = count_keys(config)
    if total_keys > 1000
      errors << "Configuration has too many keys (#{total_keys}). Maximum: 1000"
    end

    errors
  end

  def validate_depth_and_types(obj, max_depth:, current_depth: 0, current_path: "")
    errors = []

    if current_depth > max_depth
      errors << "Configuration nesting too deep at '#{current_path}' (max depth: #{max_depth})"
      return errors
    end

    case obj
    when Hash
      obj.each do |key, value|
        # Keys must be strings
        unless key.is_a?(String)
          errors << "Non-string key found at '#{current_path}': #{key.class}"
          next
        end

        # Validate key format (alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens, dots)
        unless key.match?(/\A[a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.]+\z/)
          errors << "Invalid key format at '#{current_path}': '#{key}'. Only alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens, and dots allowed."
          next
        end

        child_path = current_path.empty? ? key : "#{current_path}.#{key}"
        errors.concat(validate_depth_and_types(value, max_depth: max_depth, current_depth: current_depth + 1, current_path: child_path))
      end
    when Array
      if obj.length > 100
        errors << "Array too large at '#{current_path}' (#{obj.length} elements, max: 100)"
      end
      obj.each_with_index do |item, index|
        child_path = "#{current_path}[#{index}]"
        errors.concat(validate_depth_and_types(item, max_depth: max_depth, current_depth: current_depth + 1, current_path: child_path))
      end
    when String
      if obj.length > 10_000
        errors << "String value too long at '#{current_path}' (#{obj.length} chars, max: 10000)"
      end
    when Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass, Date, Time
      # These are all safe data-only types - allowed
    else
      errors << "Disallowed value type '#{obj.class}' at '#{current_path}'. Only strings, numbers, booleans, null, dates, arrays, and hashes are permitted."
    end

    errors
  end

  def count_keys(obj)
    case obj
    when Hash
      obj.size + obj.values.sum { |v| count_keys(v) }
    when Array
      obj.sum { |v| count_keys(v) }
    else
      0
    end
  end

  def deep_sanitize(obj)
    case obj
    when Hash
      obj.each_with_object({}) do |(key, value), result|
        sanitized_key = sanitize_string(key.to_s)
        result[sanitized_key] = deep_sanitize(value)
      end
    when Array
      obj.map { |item| deep_sanitize(item) }
    when String
      sanitize_string(obj)
    when Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass, Date, Time
      obj
    else
      # Should not reach here after validation, but be safe
      nil
    end
  end

  def sanitize_string(str)
    # Remove null bytes and strip whitespace
    str.delete("\x00").strip
  end

  def valid_content_type?(uploaded_file)
    # Check by file extension since YAML content types vary
    filename = uploaded_file.original_filename.to_s.downcase
    filename.end_with?(".yml") || filename.end_with?(".yaml")
  end

  def next_version_for(config_name)
    latest = Configuration.where(name: config_name, user: current_user)
                          .order(version: :desc)
                          .limit(1)
                          .pluck(:version)
                          .first
    (latest || 0) + 1
  end

  def authorize_access!(configuration)
    unless configuration.user_id == current_user.id || current_user.admin?
      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    end
  end

  def render_error(message, status)
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html do
        flash.now[:alert] = message
        render :new, status: status
      end
      format.json do
        render json: { error: message }, status: status
      end
    end
  end
end
